page: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is an x86_64 architecture virtual server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features 8 Intel Core Processor (Skylake) vCPUs with 8 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 512 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. No GPU resources are included, and the instance is provisioned with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. However, memory bandwidth presents a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block reads are average, uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the bottom 25%. Multi-core processing, PassMark CPU metrics, and Redis SET operations perform within the average range. Large-model LLM inference is weak, with 7B model prompt processing and text generation scoring in the bottom 10%.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that require high single-threaded performance but do not continuously saturate all cores. The bundled NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, making it suitable for static web serving, intermediate database operations, and lightweight serialization tasks. It is less suited for memory-intensive streaming or large-scale machine learning inference due to uncached memory bandwidth limitations.
description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is an x86_64 virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 8 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 512 GB NVMe SSD. It exhibits strong single-core performance, with single-threaded gzip compression placing in the top 10% and stress-ng single-core metrics in the top 25%. However, uncached memory bandwidth is a bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for large-block operations. Multi-core benchmarks, PassMark CPU scores, and Redis database operations remain average. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-threaded speed and moderate local storage, such as static web serving, lightweight database tasks, and general-purpose applications, though it is poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference or memory-intensive streaming.
og_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 512 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance with average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-8c-32gb is an x86_64 server with 8 shared Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 512 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring shared processors, fast local NVMe storage, and strong single-threaded performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 512 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core execution and database operation speeds
Weak uncached memory bandwidth and large-model LLM inference
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight databases, and general-purpose tasks
